title: mertensia lanceolata var. coriacea
intro: Mertensia lanceolata var. coriacea, also known as the woodland bluebells or western bluebells, is a perennial plant species belonging to the borage family. The plant's leaves and roots are used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mertensia lanceolata var. coriacea include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by steeping the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
